skill.name: notion-api
skill.description: Search, read, create, and update Notion pages, databases, and blocks through Bun TypeScript scripts that call the official Notion API.
skill.summary: Use Notion workspace content from Claude, Codex, or Claude Code with authenticated API scripts.
skill.author: BLTGV

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command execution, weak cryptography, and obfuscation alerts, but review found those were false positives from Markdown code fences, template literals, Notion field names, and TypeScript type declarations. The confirmed risk is legitimate but sensitive: Bun scripts read NOTION_TOKEN and send authenticated requests to the official Notion API, including operations that can create, update, archive, and read workspace content. No prompt injection attempt, credential exfiltration to non-Notion services, filesystem access, shell execution, or malicious intent was found.

medium_findings.title: Authenticated Notion Workspace Read and Write Access
medium_findings.description: The scripts use NOTION_TOKEN as a Bearer token for https://api.notion.com/v1 and expose commands that can search, read blocks, query databases, create pages, update pages, and archive pages. This is expected for a Notion integration, but publication should warn users that the token grants access to every shared Notion page or database and that prompts can trigger write operations when the user allows script execution.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed code directly reads NOTION_TOKEN and sends it to the official Notion API, then implements read and write operations. The behavior is legitimate for the skill purpose, but the access scope and write capability create a real user-data risk.

medium_findings.title: User-Supplied JSON Is Forwarded to Notion API Operations
medium_findings.description: Database filters, sorts, and page properties are parsed from command-line JSON and forwarded into Notion API request bodies. This does not create local code execution, but malformed or unintended values can change Notion content or expose broader query results within the integration permissions.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The JSON parsing and forwarding behavior is explicit in the scripts. The concern is medium confidence because it depends on user-approved command execution and Notion token scope, not hidden malicious behavior.

low_findings.title: Static Command Execution Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported external command alerts correspond to Markdown code fences, documented Bun invocation examples, and TypeScript template literals. Review found no child_process import, eval call, dynamic Function constructor, shell command construction, or local command execution beyond user-facing instructions to run the provided Bun scripts.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are documentation blocks or TypeScript string interpolation used to build Notion API URLs and messages. Repository-wide review found no shell execution APIs or command construction pattern.

low_findings.title: Static Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts are caused by ordinary Notion API field names such as id, created_time, last_edited_time, and filter direction values. Review found no hashing, encryption, signing, random token generation, or crypto library usage in the scanned files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed locations are API schema documentation or TypeScript interfaces, not cryptographic code. No cryptographic primitive usage was found during semantic review.

low_findings.title: Static Obfuscation and Reconnaissance Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The obfuscation alerts were triggered by dense Markdown reference examples and TypeScript bracket notation used for object properties. The reconnaissance alerts match normal Notion metadata fields and help text rather than host, network, or system discovery behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited code formats Notion API response objects and extracts block fields. No evidence of evasion, encoded payloads, process enumeration, host discovery, or network scanning was found.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Network Request With Bearer Token
dangerous_patterns.description: The shared client builds a URL from the official Notion API base and attaches Authorization: Bearer NOTION_TOKEN to each fetch request. This is expected for functionality, but it is a sensitive pattern because a compromised prompt could request data access through the integration permissions.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The code clearly sends a bearer token in an HTTP request to api.notion.com. It is not malicious by itself, but it is the main sensitive behavior in the skill.

dangerous_patterns.title: Workspace Mutation Commands
dangerous_patterns.description: The pages script supports creating pages, updating page properties, changing icons, and archiving or restoring pages. These are useful operations, but they can alter user data if run against the wrong page or database.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The create and update functions construct PATCH and POST requests to Notion page endpoints. The risk is clear, but it is an intended capability rather than evidence of malicious intent.

content.value_statement: Teams often need workspace knowledge without switching tools. This skill lets AI assistants search, read, query, and update Notion through authenticated API scripts.
actual_capabilities: Search accessible Notion pages and databases by query text or object type.
Retrieve page metadata and block content, including recursive child blocks.
Create Notion pages under a page or database parent.
Update page titles, icons, database properties, and archived state.
Query Notion databases with filters, sorts, and result limits.
Return consistent success, authentication, and error responses from scripts.
limitations: Requires Bun and a NOTION_TOKEN environment variable before scripts can run.
Can access only pages and databases shared with the Notion integration.
Does not manage Notion integration setup or token rotation automatically.
Does not implement pagination loops for retrieving every result from large workspaces.

use_cases.title: Find internal knowledge quickly
use_cases.description: Search workspace pages and databases, then open the most relevant Notion records for review.

use_cases.title: Maintain project records
use_cases.description: Create new project pages, update statuses, and archive completed work from assistant workflows.

use_cases.title: Analyze database content
use_cases.description: Query Notion databases with filters and sorting to summarize tasks, decisions, or customer records.

prompt_templates.title: Search Notion
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner lookup across accessible workspace content.
prompt_templates.prompt: Search my Notion workspace for pages about [topic]. Show the top results with titles and links.

prompt_templates.title: Read a Page
prompt_templates.scenario: Retrieve content from a known page.
prompt_templates.prompt: Read the Notion page with ID [page_id] and summarize its main sections and open questions.

prompt_templates.title: Query a Database
prompt_templates.prompt: Query database [database_id] for entries where [property] is [value]. Sort by last edited time and summarize the results.

prompt_templates.title: Update Workspace Records
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced write operation after user review.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Notion database entry in [database_id] titled [title] with these reviewed properties: [properties]. Confirm the target before running.

output_examples.input: Search Notion for project launch notes.
output_examples.output: A ranked list of matching Notion pages and databases, including titles, object type, links, and last edited dates.

output_examples.input: Read this page and summarize the action items.
output_examples.output: A concise summary of page blocks with grouped decisions, action items, owners, and unresolved questions.

output_examples.input: Create a task entry for the approved design review.
output_examples.output: A confirmation that the page was created, with its title, parent database, Notion link, and created timestamp.

best_practices: Use a Notion integration with the smallest workspace access needed for the task.
Confirm page IDs, database IDs, and write operations before running mutation commands.
Rotate NOTION_TOKEN regularly and remove it from shells or logs when work is complete.
anti_patterns: Do not share broad workspace roots with the integration when only one database is needed.
Do not run create, update, or archive commands from unreviewed prompt text.
Do not paste Notion integration tokens into chat messages, source files, or command history.

faq.question: What does this skill connect to?
faq.answer: It connects to the official Notion API at api.notion.com using a user-provided integration token.

faq.question: Can it read every page in my workspace?
faq.answer: No. It can read only pages and databases that are shared with the Notion integration.

faq.question: Can it change Notion content?
faq.answer: Yes. The pages script can create pages, update properties, change icons, and archive or restore pages.

faq.question: Does it store my Notion token?
faq.answer: No evidence found that it stores tokens. It reads NOTION_TOKEN from the environment when scripts run.

faq.question: Which tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as supported tools.

faq.question: What should I review before publishing?
faq.answer: Review the warning for network access, token handling, and write operations that can alter Notion workspace data.
